headline: Electric Vehicles Update - Real Stories Drive Broader Market Adoption Across America
summary: Electric vehicle (EV) adoption in the United States is expanding beyond early adopters and coastal markets, finding its place in the everyday lives of Americans nationwide. Veloz, a nonprofit organization, has launched the "Real Stories" hub on ElectricForAll.org to capture this shift, showcasing diverse first-person experiences of EV owners from various regions. The platform offers insights into how EVs are integrated into different lifestyles, from school drop-offs to long-distance travel,...

headline: What Toyota’s Earnings Mean for Tesla
summary: Toyota Motor  stock was falling after earnings, but Tesla isn’t really a car company anymore—and its shares are rising. headline: Tesla Just Ripped 19% in a Month. Is It Time to Sell in May and Go Away?
summary: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) has staged a sharp rebound. Shares closed at $411.81 on May 7, capping a roughly 19% monthly run that has erased much of the spring drawdown. Even so, the stock is down 8.4% year-to-date, which sets up a familiar tactical question for holders heading into summer: is this the moment to invoke ... Tesla Just Ripped 19% in a Month. Is It Time to Sell in May and Go Away?

headline: Apple, Intel have reached preliminary chip-making deal, WSJ reports
summary: Apple and Intel have reached a preliminary agreement for Intel to ‌manufacture some of the chips that power ‌the iPhone maker's devices, the Wall Street Journal reported on ​Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.  Apple shares were up about 2%, while Intel's stock extended ⁠gains and was last up about 14%.  The potential move could be a major win for Intel, ​which has ​been trying to revitalize ​its chip manufacturing business ‌and become a serious player in the booming chipmaking industry.

content.title: Apple, Intel have reached preliminary chip-making deal, WSJ reports
content.summary: Apple and Intel have reached a preliminary agreement for Intel to ‌manufacture some of the chips that power ‌the iPhone maker's devices, the Wall Street Journal reported on ​Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.  Apple shares were up about 2%, while Intel's stock extended ⁠gains and was last up about 14%.  The potential move could be a major win for Intel, ​which has ​been trying to revitalize ​its chip manufacturing business ‌and become a serious player in the booming chipmaking industry.

headline: Tesla’s Record Semi Order Tests Valuation And Freight Growth Expectations
summary: WattEV has agreed to purchase 370 Tesla Semi trucks from Tesla (NasdaqGS:TSLA), the largest commercial order for the electric Class 8 model so far. The deal will create the largest single electric truck deployment in California, paired with Tesla's Megawatt Charging System. The order is part of a broader build out of zero emission freight infrastructure with several charging and logistics partners involved. Tesla, known mainly for its passenger EVs and energy products, has been working to...
